steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: The left side ends with an incomplete subject (“Ann Quinby of”), and the right side begins with a direct object (“an important role...”). The blank must complete the subject with a location and provide a main verb.

steps.title: Apply the rule for independent clauses
steps.content: An independent clause requires a subject and main verb directly connected with no separating punctuation. Choice D maintains this: Ann Quinby of Kentucky played an important role forms one complete independent clause. Choices A, B, and C all violate this rule by inserting punctuation that separates the subject from its verb, creating fragments or misusing punctuation conventions. That matches choice D — D is correct.
